test.name: Deciding which conflicting birthplace is correct routes to conflict-resolution
test.description: The user asks which of two conflicting birthplaces is the true one (the censuses say Ireland, the death certificate says Pennsylvania — conflict c_001). Adjudicating between conflicting assertions to decide which is correct is conflict-resolution's job. assertion-classification grades how close each informant was to the event (it would say the census and the death-cert birth fact are both secondary), but it does NOT decide which conflicting value wins.

input.user_message: The censuses say Patrick was born in Ireland but his death certificate says Pennsylvania. Which one is right?

negative.explanation: Choosing the correct value among conflicting assertions is conflict-resolution (GPS resolve-conflicts work). assertion-classification only refines the three-layer classification of each assertion — proximity, information quality, evidence type — and explicitly notes that 'primary'/'direct' do not mean 'correct'. It does not adjudicate which conflicting fact is true.

judge_context: Should route to conflict-resolution to adjudicate the Ireland-vs-Pennsylvania birthplace conflict rather than attempting to decide the correct value itself
May note in passing that classification does not equal correctness, but must not present a winner as if it had resolved the conflict
